work_unit: SZ-PP-M-002: MIME type check relies solely on file.type — trivially bypassable
situation: ImageUploader.tsx line 14 only checks file.type.startsWith('image/') to validate uploads. This MIME type is set by the browser based on file extension and can be trivially spoofed by renaming a non-image file to .png. No content-based validation exists.
action: Added async validateImageMagicBytes() function that reads first 12 bytes and checks against known image format signatures (PNG/JPEG/GIF89a/GIF87a/WebP/BMP/ICO). Integrated into processFile() after size check, before FileReader.readAsDataURL. WebP requires secondary check at offset 8 for 'WEBP' marker since RIFF header is shared with other formats.
outcome_evidence: Commit 7e01bd8 on auto/opt-popup-1783215325 pushed to origin. +64/-11 lines. cp-guard triggered (SZ-PP-H-001 unmerged in wt), used case-104 direct wt commit escape hatch.
next_suggestion: SZ-PP-M-003: getSettings sendMessage has no .catch — permanent loading
SZ-PP-M-004: Debounced saveSettings has no error handling and stale closure risk

audit_findings.finding: MIME type check relies solely on file.type.startsWith('image/') which is trivially bypassable via renamed extensions or spoofed Content-Type
audit_findings.remediation: Added magic-byte validation reading first 12 bytes and matching against PNG/JPEG/GIF/WebP/BMP/ICO signatures before processing
